resource.name: Warning.DeleteSystem
resource.object: Deleting this system will delete stations and pograms for the system!

resource.name: Err.DeletingStationChildren
resource.object: Can't delete children of the station.

resource.name: SaveChangesLeaving
resource.object: You have unsaved changes to a program. Do you want to save the changes before leaving?

resource.name: SaveChangesRefreshing
resource.object: You have unsaved changes to a program. Do you want to save the changes before refreshing?

resource.name: ErrorRefreshingProgram
resource.object: Error while refreshing the program list.

resource.name: ErrorSavingProgram
resource.object: Error while saving any unsaved changes.

resource.name: ActualWindow.Hint
resource.object: The number of recent objects that are included in the monitoring. Example: If you set this value to 50, the window % in Product Setup - Step 2 will show the average fat membrane % for the last 50 fillets.

resource.name: WarningEnable.Hint
resource.object: Toggle between the following settings: l On l Off If you set this parameter to "Off", the machine will not give you any warnings even though the warning limit in P232 is exceeded.

resource.name: AlarmEnable.Hint
resource.object: Toggle between the following settings: l On l Off If you set this parameter to "Off", the machine will not give you any alarms even though the alarm limit in P242 is exceeded.

resource.name: WarningLimit.Hint
resource.object: If the window percentage reaches or exceeds this value, then a warning is triggered if enabled in P233. Example: l P11 (Max. fat) = 40 % l P213 (Window size) = 50 pcs. l P232 (Warning limit) = 20 % In this example, you will get a warning if more than 10 of the last 50 objects have a fat mem brane % above 40. The 10 objects corresponds to 20% of the 50 objects.

resource.name: AlarmLimit.Hint
resource.object: If the window percentage reaches or exceeds this value, then an alarm is triggered if enabled in P243. Example: l P11 (Max. fat) = 40 % l P213 (Window size) = 50 pcs. l P242 (Alarm limit) = 30 % In this example, you will get an alarm if more than 15 of the last 50 objects have a fat mem brane % above 40. The 15 objects corresponds to 30% of the 50 objects.

resource.name: TriggerLimitMax.Hint
resource.object: Objects with measurement values above this limit will be marked as having exceeded maximum report limit for the specific parameter.

resource.name: WindowSize.Hint
resource.object: The number of recent objects that are included in the monitoring.

resource.name: TriggerLimitMin.Hint
resource.object: Objects with measurement values below this limit will be marked as having not reached the minimum report limit for the specific parameter.

resource.name: CategoryLogic[0].Hint
resource.object: Decides how to logically classify pieces into the default category. Using "AND" will require all criteria to be met. Using "OR" will only require minimum one criteria to be met.

resource.name: CategoryLogic[1].Hint
resource.object: Decides how to logically classify pieces into the quality 1. Using "AND" will require all criteria to be met. Using "OR" will only require minimum one criteria to be met.

resource.name: CategoryLogic[2].Hint
resource.object: Decides how to logically classify pieces into the quality 2. Using "AND" will require all criteria to be met. Using "OR" will only require minimum one criteria to be met.

resource.name: VTWLeft.Hint
resource.object: The volume to weight ratio used for calculating the weight of an object, when only using the volume

resource.name: VTWRight.Hint
resource.object: The volume to weight ratio used for calculating the weight of an object, when only using the volume

resource.name: AlarmHysteresis.Hint
resource.object: Any warning/alarm related to the monitoring will be postponed this number of objects when pressing the yellow acknowledge button. Example: If you set this value to 20, the machine will not come up with a monitoring alarm for the first 19 instances of this specific alarm.

resource.name: PhysicalLimit.Hint
resource.object: Objects with a fat percentage above this limit will be marked as having fat in the specified zone. The window percentage is given by the percentage of objects inside the window that have values above this limit.

resource.name: LengthMax.Hint
resource.object: Objects longer than this value will be rejected without being registered in the statistics. Example: If you set this value to 800, objects that are longer than 800 mm will be rejected, and they will not be registered in the statistics either.

resource.name: LengthMin.Hint
resource.object: Objects shorter than this value will be rejected without being registered in the statistics. Example: If you set this value to 120, objects that are shorter than 120 mm will be rejected, and they will not be registered in the statistics either.

resource.name: ConveyorSpeed.Hint
resource.object: Common speed setting for all conveyors.

resource.name: ColorMax.Hint
resource.object: Objects with a color above this limit will be rejected. Example: If you set this value to 34, objects with a color above 34 will be rejected.

resource.name: ColorMin.Hint
resource.object: Objects with a color below this limit will be rejected. Example: If you set this value to 20, objects with a color below 20 will be rejected.

resource.name: Reject.WindowSize
resource.object: The number of recent objects that are included in the monitoring. Example: If you set this value to 50, the window % in Product Setup - Step 2 will show the average fat membrane % for the last 50 fillets.

resource.name: HeadFirst.Hint
resource.object: Toggle between the following settings: l Head first l Tail first The preferred setting is "Head first".

resource.name: RejectAbnormal.Hint
resource.object: Toggle between the following settings; l On l Off If you set this value to on, objects that are registered as abnormal by the color camera will be rejected, and they will not be registered in the stat istics either. This is the case if the skin side is up on a "meat side up" machine, or if the object is outside the limits that you have specified for max. and min. object length. The preferred setting is "On".

resource.name: Skin.TriggerLimitMax.Hint
resource.object: Objects with a skin area above this limit will be marked as having a skin area. The window percentage is given by the percentage of objects inside the window that have values above this limit.

resource.name: Brown.TriggerLimitMax.Hint
resource.object: Objects with a brown meat width above this limit will be marked as having wide brown meat. The window max. percentage is given by the percentage of objects inside the window that have values above this limit.

resource.name: Brown.TriggerLimitMin.Hint
resource.object: Objects with a brown meat width below this limit will be marked as having narrow brown meat. The window min percentage is given by the percentage of objects inside the window that have values below this limit.

resource.name: WithinLimits
resource.object: The value of {0} must be between {1} {3} and {2} {3}

resource.name: BoolTrue
resource.object: The value is limited to True

resource.name: BoolFalse
resource.object: The value is limeted to False

resource.name: TimeFrameSelectionView.Hint
resource.object: Select the timeframe for other dashboard components to retrieve data from

resource.name: QCScannerTopNRejectsView.Hint
resource.object: Top N Program limits dashboard component

resource.name: Hint
resource.object: Select the timeframe for other dashboard components to retrieve data from

resource.name: ExportFailedSeeLog
resource.object: Export of programs failed, see log for details.

resource.name: Failed
resource.object: Error occurred, see log for details.
